growth_records.cite: Weiss, Anna S. et al. In vitro interaction network of a synthetic gut bacterial community. The ISME Journal (2022). DOI:10.1038/s41396-021-01153-z. [PMC8941000]
growth_records.snippet: intermediate growth rate (GR > 1 h-1, ... F. plautii YL31 ...) ... Butyric acid is produced by strains F. plautii YL31 ... F. plautii YL31 also consumed lysine, indicating the ability to produce butyric acid from lysine ... F. plautii YL31
growth_records.notes: growth_rate 1.0 h-1 = lower bound of 'intermediate' bin (exact in Table S2, unavailable). Butyrate producer (also butyrate from lysine, gapseq-supported lysine fermentation pathway); consumes lysine + glutamate; produces lactate. All qualitative (MS); no numeric rates.

growth_records.agit: 300 rpm (stir bar) for monoculture growth curves; Hungate tubes otherwise
growth_records.cite: Pau Pérez Escriva et al. Distinct N and C Cross-Feeding Networks in a Synthetic Mouse Gut Consortium. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01484-21. [PMC9040589]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1: Flavonifractor plautii YL31 (Firmicutes), Max OD 0.92, Mean μ ± SD 0.49 ± 0.08. Cultures were grown in 10 mL of mBHI medium under anaerobic conditions in Hungate tubes.
growth_records.notes: Specific growth rate μ=0.49 h^-1 (±0.08) reported in Table 1 (units per hour, exponential batch growth). Doubling time calculated: td = ln2/μ = 0.6931/0.49 = 1.414 h. Max OD600 = 0.92. No quantitative uptake/secretion rates (mmol/gDW/h) given in main text; the paper characterizes exometabolome consumption/secretion only qualitatively (Fig 1B metabolic-footprint heatmap) and reports exchange amounts in C-mmol/N-mmol in supplementary tables (Table S4), not per-biomass rates, so uptake_rates/secretion_rates left empty.
